description: ADME reasoning benchmark generated from MMP analysis of ChEMBL/TDC data

tasks.prompt:
You are given a matched molecular pair for intrinsic clearance in human liver microsomes.

Compound A: COc1cccc(NC(=O)c2csc(-c3cccnc3)n2)c1
  Microsomal Intrinsic Clearance: 2.173 log CLint (µL/min/mg)

Compound B: Cc1cccc(NC(=O)c2csc(-c3cccnc3)n2)c1
  Compound B differs from A by the structural transformation: CO[*:1] → C[*:1]
  (Core/scaffold: O=C(Nc1cccc([*:1])c1)c1csc(-c2cccnc2)n1)

Questions:
1. Predict the Microsomal Intrinsic Clearance of Compound B.
2. Will this transformation result in improved metabolic stability (lower clearance) or reduced metabolic stability (higher clearance)?
3. Explain the chemical/mechanistic reasoning behind your prediction. What structural or electronic features drive this change?

evaluation_criteria.prediction_accuracy: Compare predicted value_B to actual (within 0.3 log units = good)
evaluation_criteria.direction_correct: Did the model predict the correct direction of change?
reasoning_elements: Identifies relevant metabolic soft spots or blocking groups
Correctly identifies CYP-relevant structural features for CYP tasks

Compound A: CCOc1ccc(Cc2cc([C@]34OC[C@](CO)(O3)[C@@H](O)[C@H](O)[C@H]4O)ccc2C)cc1
  Hepatocyte Intrinsic Clearance: 0.301 log CLint (µL/min/10^6 cells)

Compound B: COc1ccc(Cc2cc([C@]34OC[C@](CO)(O3)[C@@H](O)[C@H](O)[C@H]4O)ccc2C)cc1
  Compound B differs from A by the structural transformation: CC[*:1] → C[*:1]
  (Core/scaffold: Cc1ccc([C@]23OC[C@](CO)(O2)[C@@H](O)[C@H](O)[C@H]3O)cc1Cc1ccc(O[*:1])cc1)

Questions:
1. Predict the Hepatocyte Intrinsic Clearance of Compound B.
2. Will this transformation result in improved hepatocyte stability or reduced hepatocyte stability?
3. Explain the chemical/mechanistic reasoning behind your prediction. What structural or electronic features drive this change?

evaluation_criteria.prediction_accuracy: Compare predicted value_B to actual (within 0.3 log units = good)
evaluation_criteria.direction_correct: Did the model predict the correct direction of change?
reasoning_elements: Identifies relevant metabolic soft spots or blocking groups
Correctly identifies CYP-relevant structural features for CYP tasks

    R-group: CN(C)CCC[*:1]
    CYP2C9 Inhibition Potency: 4.796 pIC50
  Compound 3: COc1cccc(CNC(=O)c2ccc(-c3ccncc3)cc2)c1
    R-group: C[*:1]
    CYP2C9 Inhibition Potency: 6.921 pIC50

New compound: CCCOc1cccc(CNC(=O)c2ccc(-c3ccncc3)cc2)c1
  R-group: CCC[*:1]

Questions:
1. Predict the CYP2C9 Inhibition Potency of the new compound.
2. Explain your reasoning based on the SAR trends in the series.
3. How confident are you in this prediction? What factors could make it unreliable?

evaluation_criteria.prediction_accuracy: Within 0.3 log units of actual = good, within 0.5 = acceptable
evaluation_criteria.sar_reasoning: Does the model identify trends in the series?
evaluation_criteria.calibration: Is the stated confidence appropriate given series variance?

Relates structural features of the R-group to the property
Considers similarity to other R-groups in the series

tasks.prompt:
The following structural transformation has been observed across multiple ADME endpoints:

Transformation: CC(=O)NCC[*:1]>>c1cncc(C[*:1])c1

Observed effects:
  CYP3A4 Binary: mean delta = +0.2500 (n=20 pairs)
  CYP1A2 Binary: mean delta = +0.0870 (n=23 pairs)
  CYP2C9 Binary: mean delta = +0.0000 (n=19 pairs)
  CYP2C19 Binary: mean delta = +0.6875 (n=16 pairs)
  CYP2D6 Binary: mean delta = +0.2000 (n=10 pairs)

Questions:
